summary: Contract interfaces have no covers-graph coverage. All 6 inter-section contracts are coverage-required per file_schemas.md but zero assertions have covers fields pointing at any 'contracts/original/*.json'.
context: Contract surfaces ARE exercised behaviorally by the harness: AA.S3.4 verifies setFeedback frame shape (C-S2-S4); AA.S4.1/AA.S4.2 verify the same; AA.S5.1-AA.S5.4 verify the C-S4-S5 surface; AA.S1.3 verifies all manifest-referenced paths resolve which exercises C-S1-S2 and C-S1-S3. But no assertion has a covers field pointing at a contract file path. AA.S2.5 mentions the contract in its scenario but its covers points at section/1/acceptance.
recommendation: Defer to Arbiter for a v1.7 Principle-C strict-pass decision: either (a) extend covers fields on relevant existing assertions to also point at the contract files (each AA already maps semantically to a contract; the covers field is just narrower than the actual coverage), (b) introduce dedicated contract-coverage assertions, or (c) accept the gap on grounds that behavioral coverage exists and the schema-strict pointer is a documentation gap rather than a build gap.
